subject: Enable Logitech D-Input drivers.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Logitech RumblePad, RumblePad 2, WingMan, and Formula Vibration Feedback Wheel devices lose force-feedback/rumble capability; devices continue to work as input devices without haptic feedback.

notes:
CRITICAL: This commit is ONLY on MiSTer-v5.14 branch, NOT on MiSTer-v5.15 (stock). However, the same kconfig options (CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF=y and CONFIG_LOGIRUMBLEPAD2_FF=y) ARE enabled in v5.15's defconfig via commit 215e6e662 'Add defconfig' (2021-11-08). This represents a deliberate re-implementation: Sorgelig chose not to cherry-pick this v5.14 commit to v5.15, but instead replicated the functionality when creating the v5.15 defconfig. The commit is NOT listed in patch-provenance.md (which lists 11 defconfig commits at line 312, but not bbeff2c30). Both CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF and CONFIG_LOGIRUMBLEPAD2_FF are standard vanilla kconfig options in 6.18 (drivers/hid/Kconfig). They are enabled in stock-v5.15's linux.config and in our current linux.config. Main_MiSTer has userspace force-feedback support code (input.cpp) that depends on these drivers being present and enabled. Dropping these options would cause silent feature loss (rumble stops working, but devices remain functional as input devices). No upstream carries this specific commit because the options are standard vanilla features available in all versions since before 5.15.

sonnet-verified: PASS, no amendment. CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF=y and CONFIG_LOGIRUMBLEPAD2_FF=y both re-confirmed directly in output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config:2950-2951 (the resolved build config named in this audit's instructions). Re-derived via git show that bbeff2c30 exists ONLY on origin/MiSTer-v5.14 (git branch -a --contains confirms it is absent from MiSTer-v5.15), and that 215e6e6621ad60563c7385c671e9886f3b1d8994 ('Add defconfig', 2021-11-08, present on MiSTer-v5.15) independently introduces CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF=y and CONFIG_LOGIRUMBLEPAD2_FF=y in its initial 4187-line defconfig add -- confirming the record's 're-implementation, not cherry-pick' narrative. Both Kconfig symbols confirmed present in vanilla 6.18's drivers/hid/Kconfig (LOGITECH_FF:673, LOGIRUMBLEPAD2_FF:687), both depends on HID_LOGITECH + select INPUT_FF_MEMLESS, unrenamed since 5.15. Main_MiSTer citations re-verified by direct grep: input.cpp:1503 (`int has_rumble;`), :4871 (`ioctl(fd, EVIOCRMFF, ...)`), :4881 (`fef->type = FF_RUMBLE;`), :5228 (`test_bit(FF_RUMBLE, ff_features)`) all match exactly as cited.
